openmeter/portal/adapter.purpose: JWT-backed stateless implementation of portal.Service that issues and validates short-lived HS256 tokens scoped to namespace, subject, and optional meter slug allowlist. ListTokens and InvalidateToken are intentionally unimplemented stubs returning GenericNotImplementedError — no database backing by design.

patterns.name: Config-validated constructor
patterns.description: New(Config) validates all fields via Config.Validate() before constructing; always returns (portal.Service, error) — never panics on bad config.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (portal.Service, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err } ... }

patterns.name: Interface compliance assertion
patterns.description: var _ portal.Service = (*adapter)(nil) at package level — compile-time proof the struct satisfies the interface.

patterns.name: JWT claims struct embeds jwt.RegisteredClaims
patterns.description: JTWPortalTokenClaims embeds jwt.RegisteredClaims and adds Namespace, Id, AllowedMeterSlugs. Always parse with ParseWithClaims into this type, never into map[string]interface{}.
patterns.example: type JTWPortalTokenClaims struct { jwt.RegisteredClaims; Namespace string; AllowedMeterSlugs []string }

patterns.name: Strict JWT parse options — never relax
patterns.description: Validate uses WithStrictDecoding, WithExpirationRequired, WithIssuer, and WithValidMethods. Do not remove any when extending validation.

patterns.name: Noop adapter for disabled/test contexts
patterns.description: NewNoop() returns a full no-op portal.Service where every method returns models.NewGenericNotImplementedError — not nil error. Use in Wire when portal is disabled.
patterns.example: func (a *noopAdapter) CreateToken(ctx, input) (*portal.PortalToken, error) { return nil, models.NewGenericNotImplementedError(fmt.Errorf("noop adapter")) }

key_file_guides.role: Config struct, Validate(), New() constructor, adapter struct definition.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Secret is []byte internally; Config.Secret is string — converted in New(). Expire=0 is rejected by Validate(). Always call New() and check the error.

key_file_guides.role: CreateToken (JWT sign) and Validate (JWT parse + claims extraction); ListTokens and InvalidateToken stub out with NotImplemented.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The raw token string is only exposed in the CreateToken response (Token field NOT populated in toAPIPortalToken) — adding it elsewhere is a security risk.

key_file_guides.role: Full no-op portal.Service for tests and disabled-portal wiring.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All methods must return models.NewGenericNotImplementedError, not nil — callers distinguish 'not implemented' from success. No business logic here.

anti_patterns: Storing token state in a database here — this adapter is intentionally stateless.
Relaxing JWT validation options (removing WithExpirationRequired, WithStrictDecoding, WithValidMethods, or WithIssuer).
Populating the Token field inside toAPIPortalToken — token string must only appear in CreateToken response.
Calling New() without checking the returned error.
Adding business logic to the noop adapter — it must remain a pure stub returning NotImplementedError.

decisions.decision: Stateless JWT tokens with no DB persistence for ListTokens/InvalidateToken.
decisions.rationale: Short-lived portal metering tokens do not require revocation in the current design; unimplemented methods return NotImplemented to make gaps explicit.

decisions.decision: HS256 with a shared secret rather than asymmetric signing.
decisions.rationale: Portal tokens are validated by the same server that issues them, so symmetric signing suffices and avoids key distribution complexity.

code_examples.scenario: Create and validate a portal token

openmeter/portal/authenticator.purpose: Chi middleware that authenticates HTTP requests against v1 OpenAPI security requirements, dispatching to verifyPortalToken for PortalTokenAuth-scoped routes and injecting the validated subject into context via a typed context key.

patterns.name: OpenAPI-driven security dispatch
patterns.description: getSecurityRequirements reads per-operation security from the swagger spec (falling back to global); validateSecurityRequirements iterates alternatives and returns on first success — mirrors OpenAPI OR semantics.
patterns.example: for _, sr := range securityRequirements { r, err = a.validateSecurityRequirement(sr, w, r); if err == nil { return r, nil } }

patterns.name: Subject injected via typed context key
patterns.description: Authenticated subject stored under AuthenticatorSubjectSessionKey (typed AuthenticatorContextKey). Retrieve with GetAuthenticatedSubject(ctx) — never the raw string key.

patterns.name: Security scheme matched by prefix split
patterns.description: getAuthenticatorFunc splits api.PortalTokenAuthScopes on '.' and matches by first segment. New auth schemes require a new case in this switch.

patterns.name: Nil security requirement means unauthenticated route
patterns.description: If getSecurityRequirements returns nil (no matching route/operation), the middleware calls next directly without authentication.
patterns.example: if sr == nil { next.ServeHTTP(w, r); return }

key_file_guides.role: Entire authenticator: struct, constructor, Chi middleware factory, portal token verification, OpenAPI security resolution.
key_file_guides.watch_for: verifyPortalToken extracts meterSlug from chi.URLParam — a route param rename silently stops enforcing slug allowlists. AllowedMeterSlugs uses slices.Contains; empty slice means all slugs allowed. Auth failures must always be 401 via models.NewStatusProblem.

anti_patterns: Adding security schemes by hardcoding plain string names instead of api.* constants split on '.'.
Calling portal.Validate outside verifyPortalToken — validation must flow through the middleware.
Skipping the AllowedMeterSlugs check when meterSlug is empty or the route has no slug param.
Returning 200 or 500 for auth failures — unauthorized must always be 401 via models.NewStatusProblem.

decisions.decision: Security requirements read from the swagger spec at request time rather than compiled into route handlers.
decisions.rationale: Keeps auth policy co-located with the OpenAPI spec; new endpoints inherit security without changing middleware code.

openmeter/portal/httphandler.purpose: v1 HTTP handler package (package httpdriver) for portal token endpoints — implements the httptransport.Handler pattern for CreateToken, ListTokens, InvalidateToken, delegating to portal.Service and meter.Service for cross-domain meter slug validation.

patterns.name: Composite Handler interface with sub-interfaces
patterns.description: Top-level Handler embeds TokenHandler; each operation returns a typed handler alias. var _ Handler = (*handler)(nil) enforces compliance.
patterns.example: type TokenHandler interface { CreateToken() CreateTokenHandler; ListTokens() ListTokensHandler; InvalidateToken() InvalidateTokenHandler }

patterns.name: httptransport.NewHandler / NewHandlerWithArgs per endpoint
patterns.description: Each endpoint is a decoder + operation + ResponseEncoder + options closure. Params-bearing endpoints use NewHandlerWithArgs; param-less use NewHandler.

patterns.name: Namespace resolved at the start of every decoder
patterns.description: resolveNamespace(ctx) wraps namespaceDecoder.GetNamespace; returns HTTP 500 on failure. Always call at the very start of every decoder before other logic.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return ..., err }

patterns.name: Domain input type as request type alias
patterns.description: Request types reuse domain input structs directly rather than duplicating fields, keeping the handler thin.

patterns.name: Token field added post-mapping in CreateToken only
patterns.description: toAPIPortalToken omits the raw JWT string; CreateToken manually sets portalToken.Token = token.Token after the mapping helper.

patterns.name: WithOperationName appended on every handler
patterns.description: httptransport.AppendOptions always appends WithOperationName for OTel span naming — never omit.

key_file_guides.role: Handler/TokenHandler interfaces, handler struct, New() constructor, resolveNamespace helper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New() takes variadic HandlerOption — callers pass error handler and tracer options; forgetting them silences errors and breaks OTel tracing.

key_file_guides.role: All three endpoint implementations: ListTokens, CreateToken, InvalidateToken.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreateToken validates AllowedMeterSlugs against the live meter list via meterService.ListMeters in the operation phase, not decode. InvalidateToken returns 204 (EmptyResponseEncoder), not 200.

key_file_guides.role: toAPIPortalToken maps portal.PortalToken to api.PortalToken; sets Expired when ExpiresAt is in the past.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Token field is intentionally NOT mapped here — set it explicitly in CreateToken after toAPIPortalToken; adding it here is a security risk.

anti_patterns: Returning the raw JWT token string via toAPIPortalToken — set it manually only in the CreateToken operation.
Adding database calls directly in handlers — delegate to portal.Service or meter.Service.
Omitting WithOperationName from AppendOptions — breaks OTel span names.
Using context.Background() instead of the request context in decoder or operation closures.

decisions.decision: Meter slug validation in CreateToken operation rather than inside portal.Service.
decisions.rationale: portal.Service is JWT-only and has no meter knowledge; cross-domain validation belongs in the HTTP layer that has both services.

decisions.decision: Request type aliases to domain input structs.
decisions.rationale: Avoids duplicating field definitions; keeps the handler thin with the domain type as the single source of truth.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new portal endpoint following the existing handler pattern
